metadata.title: Why a Blinding Red Lamp Releases Fewer Electrons Than a Dim UV Torch
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = photon above threshold / electrons ejected; CRIMSON = photon below threshold / no electrons regardless of intensity
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no stopping-potential voltage algebra, no Einstein-equation derivation, no Compton, no Millikan history. Wonder register.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: A million-watt red spotlight aimed at sodium metal releases exactly zero electrons. A dim UV penlight releases them instantly. Color is the switch. Brightness is only the rate.

card.copy: Why a Blinding Red Lamp Releases Fewer Electrons Than a Dim UV Torch

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A million-watt red spotlight aimed at sodium metal releases exactly zero electrons. A dim UV penlight releases them instantly. Color is the switch. Brightness i

beats.narration_text: Classical wave theory says light transfers energy continuously — enough intensity should always free an electron eventually. The experiment says otherwise, flatly and repeatedly. Below a threshold frequency: nothing. Above it: electrons immediately.

production_viz.label: classical: intensity should work; experiment: frequency is the switch

beats.narration_text: Why does color matter and intensity not? Classical theory says more intensity means more energy delivered to the surface — electrons should be freed eventually. But they only emerge above a threshold frequency. Why?

card.copy: Why does frequency control electron emission, while intensity only controls the rate — not whether electrons appear at all?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Why does color matter and intensity not? Classical theory says more intensity means more energy delivered to the surface — electrons should be freed eventually.

beats.narration_text: Light arrives as photons. Each carries energy E equals h times nu. A photon either has enough energy to kick one electron over the work function barrier, or it doesn't. No accumulation across multiple photons — one photon, one electron, instant decision.

production_viz.label: one photon hits one electron: either over barrier or not

beats.narration_text: Sodium work function: 2.28 electron-volts. Green photon at 546 nanometers: 2.27 electron-volts — just below. No electron, no matter how many green photons land. UV photon at 300 nanometers: 4.13 electron-volts — well above. Each UV photon ejects one electron with 1.85 eV of kinetic energy.

production_viz.label: threshold line at 2.28eV, green 2.27eV below, UV 4.13eV above

beats.narration_text: The kinetic energy of the emitted electron is KE equals h-nu minus phi — photon energy minus work function. This doesn't depend on intensity at all. Only on frequency. Brighter light means more electrons per second, but each one has the same kinetic energy.

production_viz.label: KE = hnu - phi, linear in frequency, independent of intensity

beats.narration_text: This was Einstein's 1905 paper — the one that won him the Nobel Prize, not relativity. He took Planck's quantization seriously and applied it to light itself. Light is not a continuous wave for energy transfer; it is discrete. Each quantum acts alone.

production_viz.label: photon energy discrete, E=hnu, single quantum acts alone

beats.narration_text: Shine green light at 546 nanometers — 2.27 electron-volts — at 1 kilowatt onto sodium. Zero electrons, forever. The photon energy is 0.01 eV below the threshold. Now switch to a 1 milliwatt UV source at 300 nanometers. Electrons appear immediately. Frequency is the switch; intensity is just the rate.

production_viz.label: 1kW green = zero electrons; 1mW UV = electrons immediately

beats.narration_text: The photoelectric effect established that light has particle-like energy quantization. The wave nature — interference, diffraction — coexists with the particle nature — discrete photon energy. This duality is not a contradiction. It is the nature of quantum fields.

production_viz.label: wave: interference pattern; particle: discrete E=hnu per photon

beats.narration_text: E equals h-nu per photon. One photon, one electron. Below threshold: nothing, no matter the intensity. Above threshold: kinetic energy equals h-nu minus phi for each electron. Frequency is the key, not brightness.

card.copy: E=hν per photon. Below threshold: zero. Above threshold: KE=hν−φ. Frequency decides. Intensity sets the rate.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: E equals h-nu per photon. One photon, one electron. Below threshold: nothing, no matter the intensity. Above threshold: kinetic energy equals h-nu minus phi for

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
